subject: Human Resources
title: Emotional and Social Skills
description: A micro-lesson that makes Emotional and Social Skills usable.

hook.text: A hospital replaces receptionists with kiosks. Patient satisfaction drops.

buildup.text: As AI handles technical tasks, human skills—empathy, communication, persuasion, and collaboration—become more valuable, not less.

discovery.text: Deloitte predicts two-thirds of all jobs will be 'soft-skill intensive' by 2030—social and emotional capabilities leading demand.

twist.text: We call them 'soft skills'—but they're the hardest to develop. Technical skills take weeks. Empathy and leadership take years.

climax.text: The most future-proof workers combine technical literacy with emotional intelligence—understanding both code and humans.

punchline.text: Machines get smarter at hard skills. That makes soft skills the hardest to replace.

quiz.question: What does Deloitte predict about soft-skill intensive jobs by 2030?
options: Two-thirds of all jobs will require strong soft skills
Only 10% of jobs will need them
